---
title: "Preface"
---

# Welcome {.unnumbered}

This book introduces computational text analysis for researchers in media and communication studies. It assumes basic familiarity with R but no prior experience with natural language processing or text mining.

## What You Will Learn

By the end of this book you will be able to:

- Collect and prepare text data from news archives, social media APIs, and web sources
- Apply tidytext and quanteda workflows to preprocess and explore corpora
- Perform sentiment analysis, topic modeling, and automated content analysis
- Interpret and communicate results to social science audiences

## How to Use This Book

Each chapter mixes explanation with **interactive code blocks** — you can run and modify R code directly in your browser without installing anything. Look for cells marked with the {{< fa play >}} button.

For deeper work, clone the repository and use the provided `renv` lockfile to reproduce the exact package environment:

```r
# In R, after cloning
renv::restore()
```

## Software

The book was built with:

- [R](https://cran.r-project.org/) (≥ 4.3)
- [Quarto](https://quarto.org/) (≥ 1.4)
- [quarto-live](https://r-wasm.github.io/quarto-live/) for in-browser interactivity
- [renv](https://rstudio.github.io/renv/) for reproducible package management

See @sec-getting-started for installation instructions.

## Conventions

- Code that runs live in the browser uses `{webr}` chunks.
- Code meant to be run locally uses standard `{r}` chunks with pre-computed output.
- Margin notes provide tips and cross-references.

## Acknowledgements

*Add acknowledgements here.*
